summary: A rectangular multi-story fortified building with two tall corner towers and a flat dark roof. Exterior walls are primarily gray stonebrick with horizontal banding and rows of small windows; raised stepped terraces on the short side create a layered façade. Surrounding the main footprint is a low cobblestone block perimeter with spaced stubs and glowstone lighting on corners and roof edges.

shape.overall_form: rectangular block with two tall square towers at opposite short corners and a stepped terrace on one short side
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (towers at both short ends, terraces appear mirrored-ish)

elements: small recessed rectangular windows in rows (mostly 1x1 glass panes)
two tall square towers at short ends topped by wide capped roofs
flat dark main roof with low parapet and glowstone corner lights
stepped terrace / balcony protrusions on one short side with staircase-like layers
low cobblestone block border around building footprint with spaced stubs
rebuild_hints: Use a rectangular stonebrick volume ~19x29 with height ~16 for the main three floors; add a 3-4 block tall parapet and a flat dark (netherbrick) roof inset slightly from the parapet.
Place two square towers (4-5 blocks wide) on each short corner, extending ~6 blocks above the main roof; cap towers with stacked slab/stone edging and a dark roof slab layer with glowstone center.
Create horizontal banding by recessing every 4 blocks and adding a 1-block high stonebrick trim; place rows of single 1x1 windows aligned across each floor.
Add a stepped, offset terrace on one short side: three decreasing ledges projecting from the wall aligned with floor levels.
Surround the building footprint with a 1-block-high cobblestone stub perimeter spaced uniformly; add glowstone posts at major corners and near tower bases.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the dark roof (interpreted as netherbrick) and tower cap edging (smooth stone slab) are uncertain from images.
Precise interior layout, door locations, and whether windows are panes or full glass blocks are not visible.
Dimensions are approximate integers derived from visible block counts and aerial view; actual counts may differ by several blocks.
